wiki.summary: "Trap Queen" is a song by rapper & singer Fetty Wap, it was first released independently on April 22, 2014 following it's online premier the month before in March 2014 before it was re-released on December 15, 2014 as the debut & lead single to his self-titled debut album, Fetty Wap. Trap Queen is also included to his Up Next mixtape uploaded to his Soundcloud account. He sold the mixtape in his hometown Paterson, NJ & it got popular in his hometown. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Trap Queen" is a song by rapper & singer Fetty Wap, it was first released independently on April 22, 2014 following it's online premier the month before in March 2014 before it was re-released on December 15, 2014 as the debut & lead single to his self-titled debut album, Fetty Wap. Trap Queen is also included to his Up Next mixtape uploaded to his Soundcloud account. He sold the mixtape in his hometown Paterson, NJ & it got popular in his hometown. Trap Queen ranked at #4 on Billboard's Year-End list of 2015 & is currently certified 12× Platinum by the RIAA, though it is eligible for Double Diamond status. Written by Fetty Wap and produced by Tony Fadd of RGF Productions, Trap Queen is a melodic lofi hip-hop, trap, R&B, and pop rap song. Fetty sings most of the song's verses, along with its chorus, in a gritty, melodic delivery reminiscent of contemporary Southern hip-hop artists such as Gucci Mane, Future, Rich Homie Quan & Young Thug. Fetty's sung vocals, particularly during the song's chorus, are digitally altered. One additional verse, following the first iteration of the chorus, is rapped in a more straightforward, traditional style. The song's beat is largely characterized by its trap percussion and synthesized chords. Lyrically, Trap Queen is a giddy, affectionate tribute to a girlfriend and "partner in crime" whom Fetty calls his trap queen. Fetty has clarified that the song was written about an ex he had been dating and dealing crack cocaine with during his own time in the trap. In it's lyrics, Fetty proclaims his love for his girlfriend, fondly recalling counting money, going shopping, going to the strip club, getting high, and "cooking pies" with her. The lyrics also express an aspirational quality, with Fetty and his girlfriend setting a goal to buy matching Lamborghinis with the money they earn together. Fetty conceived Trap Queen in late 2013 and recorded the song the following March after hearing what would become it's backing track, which had been produced by Tony Fadd, a musician from Belarus. The backing track was released on Matsulevich's website for free download. It was the first recording on which Fetty had sung, as he had been anxious to try "something different". The original studio recording consisted of three sung verses that Fetty had freestyled in the melody he had written. The recording was uploaded to SoundCloud shortly after in March 2014, where it soon began amassing plays. Although Fetty had a feeling that Trap Queen was a potential hit even before recording the song, particularly when he first heard the beat, his suspicions that it could reach an audience outside his own region were confirmed later as he noticed its growing popularity on social media websites like Twitter and Instagram; meanwhile, its play count on SoundCloud had increased to over a million within its first months online. To take advantage of the increased attention the song was receiving, a revised version was subsequently made for radio which shortened the total runtime and included an additional rapped verse following the first iteration of the chorus. In November 2014, Fetty and RGF Productions partnered with Lyor Cohen's label 300 Entertainment to give Trap Queen a full-fledged commercial release & later the 2 labels jointly issued the most recent edit of the single to digital retailers. The song's accompanying music video was directed by RGF Productions head Nitt Da Gritt & uploaded to Fetty Wap's YouTube channel on August 7, 2014, and has received more than 961 million views as of 2026. Set in Fetty's home neighborhood in Paterson, New Jersey, the video depicts Fetty and his onscreen girlfriend at their apartment and amidst a group of neighbors partying. Fetty first arrives to his home in a black BMW convertible, where he is greeted by his girlfriend & joins her inside, where many others are gathered. The two are shown counting money and smoking together, and one shot shows Fetty's girlfriend baking a pie. Later, Fetty's girlfriend drives a white car to meet Fetty on a street at his black convertible, where Fetty kisses her and hands her a stack of dollar bills before they walk away together. The remainder of the video shows Wap socializing with neighbors at an outdoor gathering. The video is interspersed with shots of Fetty outdoors singing the song while a crowd of neighbors dances and sings along behind him. Trap Queen received much of its early attention through social media websites like Twitter, Vine & Instagram, where Fetty Wap and his team had directed early promotional efforts for the song. A major early source of exposure through such media was the rapper Bobby Shmurda, at the time enjoying the success of his own viral hit single Hot N*gga (or "Hot Boy" in it's censored version), who posted a video to his Instagram account on October 16, 2014, in which he sang along to Trap Queen. "Every girl wants to be a trap queen now, but how you gonna cook crack when you can't even cook breakfast?" would eventually become a popular social media meme inspired by the song. Toward the end of 2014 and early in 2015, the promotional focus for Wap and his team shifted toward performing live and securing mainstream airplay. By November 2014, the song had begun amassing airplay within the tri-state area from stations like Hot 97; in particular, 300 Entertainment co-founder Todd Moscowitz credited that station's Funkmaster Flex as an important early champion of the song at radio. On February 12, 2015, Kanye West brought Fetty onstage as a surprise guest at the first annual Roc City Classic concert hosted by Roc Nation and Power 105.1 in Madison Square Park. Kanye proclaimed Trap Queen his "favorite song" of the moment, and JAY-Z & Beyoncé were seen dancing in the audience together as Fetty performed the song. Fetty also performed the song at his own shows, including a showcase at South by Southwest, along with further guest performances at shows for Chris Brown & Trey Songz. Trap Queen was also the final song performed in closing set at the 2015 XXL Freshmen show. In his television debut, Fetty performed Trap Queen at the 2015 MTV Movie Awards with Fall Out Boy on April 12, 2015. The band opened the performance with part of their own hit single "Centuries" before segueing into Fetty performance of Trap Queen, for which they provided instrumental and backing vocal accompaniment. Fetty also performed the song as a musical guest on 2 late-night talk shows: first on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon May 5, accompanied by The Roots, then on Jimmy Kimmel Live! June 9, accompanied by Brooklyn band Phony Ppl. He also performed the song as a guest on Nick Cannon Presents Wild 'n Out June 24 and as the closing performer on the BET Awards 2015. The song was notable for being used in memes related to Jerry Seinfeld's ephebophilic relationship with Shoshanna Lonstein. The usage of the "1738" tagline is in correlation to each of their ages when they first met (Lonstein being 17 and Seinfeld being 38). The meme was spawned primarily on Twitter and gained popularity in 2024, where users would claim something connecting Seinfeld to Fetty Wap, and would ask the viewer to search up phrases like "Seinfeld 1738". Trap Queen debuted at #86 on the Billboard Hot 100 on the week of February 7, 2015, after seven weeks charting, the song reached the top 10 for the first time, rising from #12 to #10 on the week of March 28, 2015, largely on the strength of it's streaming activity and digital download sales. It became Fetty's first top 10 single on the Hot 100, it slowly climbed up the Hot 100 as radio airplay grew until it reached it's peak of #2 for 3 consecutive weeks in May 2015, held from the top spot by Wiz Khalifa's See You Again featuring Charlie Puth that went on to spend 12 weeks at #1. Trap Queen became his highest charting song & notably spent time in 9 out of the 10 spots in the Hot 100's top 10, with the #1 spot being the only outlier. Over the course of its run, Trap Queen had also reached #1 on Hot Rap Songs and #2 on Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs, rising to its peak on the latter chart concurrently with taking over the #1 position on R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay. By the end of 2015, Trap Queen had sold 2,730,000 digital downloads domestically. Trap Queen generated the most on-demand streams in 2015, according to Nielsen Music, with a combined total of 616.46 million audio and video streams. It was one of five songs to surpass 400 million in on-demand streams during the year. On the week of January 26, 2026, Trap Queen re-entered the Billboard Hot 100 for the first time in almost 11 years at #37, this was due to Fetty Wap being released from jail 3 weeks earlier.
